title: Create Proper File Structure
prompt:
# TASK 1: Create Proper File Structure ## CONTEXT **Current state:** Basic Electron app with placeholder Three.js code all in index.html. **Relevant files:** src/renderer/index.html contains inline JS that needs to be refactored. ## OBJECTIVE Refactor the code into proper separate files and create the full directory structure. ## IMPLEMENTATION ### Step 1: Create directories Run: mkdir -p demo ### Step 2: Create src/renderer/styles.css Create this file with: ```css * { margin: 0; padding: 0; box-sizing: border-box; } html, body { height: 100%; overflow: hidden; font-family: -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, sans-serif; } body { background: #1e1e1e; color: #ffffff; } #viewport { width: 100%; height: 100%; } ``` ### Step 3: Create src/renderer/renderer.js Extract the JavaScript from index.html into this new file. Keep the ES module format. The file should: - Import THREE - Set up scene, camera, renderer - Add grid and axes - Add lights - Have animation loop - Handle resize ### Step 4: Simplify src/renderer/index.html Replace with: ```html ClaudeCAD
``` **Files to create:** src/renderer/styles.css, src/renderer/renderer.js **Files to modify:** src/renderer/index.html ## VERIFY ```bash npm start ``` - [ ] App opens with dark background - [ ] Three.js renders (grid, axes, test cube visible) - [ ] No errors in DevTools console ## NEXT TASK Replace placeholder controls with OrbitControls

title: Add OrbitControls
prompt:
# TASK 2: Add OrbitControls

## CONTEXT
**Current state:** Three.js renders with custom placeholder mouse controls.
**Relevant files:** src/renderer/renderer.js

## OBJECTIVE
Replace the janky custom mouse handling with proper Three.js OrbitControls.

## IMPLEMENTATION

### Step 1: Add OrbitControls import
At top of renderer.js, add:
```javascript
import { OrbitControls } from 'three/addons/controls/OrbitControls.js';
```
If that path fails, try: 'three/examples/jsm/controls/OrbitControls.js'

### Step 2: DELETE all custom mouse code
Remove these things entirely:
- The `isDragging` variable
- The `spherical` object
- The `updateCamera()` function
- ALL the event listeners for mousedown, mouseup, mouseleave, mousemove, wheel

### Step 3: Create OrbitControls after renderer
Add this code after creating the renderer:
```javascript
const controls = new OrbitControls(camera, renderer.domElement);
controls.enableDamping = true;
controls.dampingFactor = 0.05;
controls.target.set(0, 0, 0);
controls.mouseButtons = {
  LEFT: THREE.MOUSE.ROTATE,
  MIDDLE: THREE.MOUSE.DOLLY,
  RIGHT: THREE.MOUSE.PAN
};
```

### Step 4: Update camera for Z-up
```javascript
camera.position.set(70, -70, 50);
camera.up.set(0, 0, 1);
```

### Step 5: Add controls.update() to animation loop
```javascript
function animate() {
  requestAnimationFrame(animate);
  controls.update();
  renderer.render(scene, camera);
}
```

**Files to modify:** src/renderer/renderer.js

## VERIFY
```bash
npm start
```
- [ ] Left mouse drag ROTATES view around origin
- [ ] Right mouse drag PANS the view
- [ ] Scroll wheel ZOOMS in/out
- [ ] Camera movement is SMOOTH (damping)
- [ ] Z-axis (blue) points UP

## NEXT TASK
Add header bar with View dropdown

title: Add Header with View Dropdown
Add Chat Panel and Status Bar
Make Chat Panel Resizable
Add Loading Spinner
Remove Test Cube and Finalize
prompt:
# TASK 7: Remove Test Cube and Finalize

## CONTEXT
**Current state:** Full UI complete with test cube still visible.
**Relevant files:** src/renderer/renderer.js

## OBJECTIVE
Remove test cube, clean up, prepare for Phase 2.

## IMPLEMENTATION

### Step 1: Remove test cube from renderer.js
Delete these lines (the test cube code):
```javascript
const geometry = new THREE.BoxGeometry(20, 20, 10);
const material = new THREE.MeshStandardMaterial({ ... });
const cube = new THREE.Mesh(geometry, material);
cube.position.set(10, 10, 5);
scene.add(cube);
```

### Step 2: Keep the lights
Don't delete the ambient light and directional light - needed for future models.

### Step 3: Add mesh loading placeholder
Add near end of file:
```javascript
// Mesh loading - Phase 2
let currentMesh = null;

function loadMesh(path) {
  console.log('loadMesh() - will be implemented in Phase 2');
}

window.loadMesh = loadMesh;
```

### Step 4: Update console message
Change the success log to:
```javascript
console.log('ClaudeCAD Phase 1 Complete');
```

**Files to modify:** src/renderer/renderer.js

## VERIFY
```bash
npm start
```
- [ ] NO CUBE - just empty grid and axes
- [ ] Grid horizontal on XY plane
- [ ] Blue axis (Z) points UP
- [ ] Header with title and View dropdown
- [ ] View presets work
- [ ] Chat panel with input
- [ ] Chat panel resizable
- [ ] Status bar shows Ready
- [ ] Press L - spinner appears/disappears
- [ ] Console: 'ClaudeCAD Phase 1 Complete'

## PHASE 1 COMPLETE
Ready for Phase 2: Python Backend
